title: Extra Functionality
text: rtf_bookmark function inserts RTF bookmark around table figure content RTF body. Given bookmark name, e.g., mybkmk, RTF tags {\\*\\bkmkstart mybkmk} {\\*\\bkmkend mybkmk} added within RTF code define bookmarked area. useful auto-linking RTFs within MS Word, e.g., context Clinical Study Reports (CSRs) external table figure RTFs may auto-linked. rtf_bookmark function also allows specify whether bookmark include table figure title. inc_title TRUE, bookmark placed includes title table figure, otherwise, FALSE, bookmark include table figure content. inc_title FALSE, important note function uses string matching find table figure content begins: Table content: first occurrence trowd Figure content: first occurrence pict

title: Extra Functionality
text: rtf_insert_heading function allows users transform table figure title specified heading style. accomplished first inserting heading definition stylesheet RTF using rtf_insert_stylesheet_headings(). Next, rtf_insert_heading function uses string match find title RTF body inserts corresponding heading level tag (e.g., \s1 heading level 1) title text. important note function uses string matching find title RTF body searching first occurrence specified font family bold tags. example, using Calibri font, function search first occurrence \f6\b insert heading level tag transform title, e.g., \s1\f6\b.

title: Extra Functionality
text: rtf_insert_stylesheet_headings function inserts stylesheet within RTF defines heading levels 1 4: {\stylesheet{\s1 heading 1;}{\s2 heading 2;}{\s3 heading 3;}{\s4 heading 4;}} allows users specify heading level table figure titles level 4.

title: Extra Functionality
text: rtf_tabstop function adds right-aligned tab stop first line RTF page header footer. useful formatting header footer content, aligning page numbers timestamps right. function sets tabstop location specified twips (1 inch = 1440 twips) using \tqr\tx n_twips tag, n_twips replaced actual twips value; little arithmetic needed align tab right margin. type argument specifies whether modify header footer. assumed first line header footer already contains tab character, expected, instance: result page header 2 lines: Line 1: “Organization” followed tab stop, “Page X Y” (X current page number Y total number pages). Line 2: “Study Analysis”. Similarly, footer, first line snapshot information followed tab character date RTF creation.
code: hdr <- c("My Organization \\tab Page \\pagenumber of \\pagefield",          "My Study Analysis")

title: Outputting Figures
text: vignette demonstrates output figures RTF using fig_to_rtf function. fig_to_rtf function takes plot object input outputs RTF, format fairly opinionated, see.

title: Outputting Figures
text: First load package format example data. Next, ’ll use fig_to_rtf function defaults, providing minimally required arguments figure size arguments. also possible output figures created using base R plotting capabilities. can record plot using recordPlot(), function grDevices packages, set class, output. example shown . information inputs, check function documentation ?fig_to_rtf.
code: library(ggplot2) library(r2rtfExtras)  # Create ggplot object plt <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(x=mpg, y=disp)) +   geom_point() +   theme_classic() fig_to_rtf(plot_obj        = plt,            file_name       = "MyFigure.rtf",             figure_title    = "Figure 1. My figure",            footer_snapshot = "Analysis 2025",            path_program    = "C:\\\\program\\\\path\\\\code.r",            fig_width       = 7,             fig_height      = 3.2) plot(mtcars$mpg, mtcars$disp, pch=16) plt_record <- recordPlot() class(plt_record) <- c(class(plt_record), "ggplot")  fig_to_rtf(plot_obj        = plt_record,            file_name       = "MyRecordedFigure.rtf",             figure_title    = "Figure 1. My recorded figure",            footer_snapshot = "Analysis 2025",            path_program    = "C:\\\\program\\\\path\\\\code.r",            fig_width       = 7,             fig_height      = 5)

title: Outputting Tables
text: vignette demonstrates output dataframes RTF using df_to_rtf function. df_to_rtf function takes dataframe input outputs RTF, format fairly opinionated, see.

title: Outputting Tables
text: First load package format example data. Next, ’ll use df_to_rtf function defaults, providing minimally required arguments. enforced opinions output: page header right-aligned page number page footer left-aligned snapshot right-aligned timestamp, well rows paths program output. also notice output: page header largely empty includes page number. table column names informative. page footer Output Name less desirable. Let’s improve issues generate informative RTF: Much better! Finally, things readily apparent. default, df_to_rtf function add following: stylesheet allows setting table title heading style level 1 4. table title set heading style level 2 default recognized Word. Bookmark start end tags around table. useful auto-linking within documents.
code: library(r2rtfExtras)  df       <- mtcars[1:10,] df$Model <- row.names(df) df       <- df[c("Model", "mpg", "cyl", "disp", "hp")] df_to_rtf(df              = df,           file_name       = "MyTable.rtf",           table_title     = "Table 1. MTCARS Dataset",           footer_snapshot = "Henderson and Velleman (1981)",            path_program    = "C:\\\\program\\\\path\\\\tables.Rmd") df_to_rtf(df              = df,           file_name       = "MyTableImproved.rtf",           org             = "My Organization",           header_page     = "My MTCARS Analysis",           table_title     = "Table 1. MTCARS Dataset",           header_table1   = c("Model", "Miles Per Gallon", "Cylinders",                                "Displacement", "Horsepower"),            footer_snapshot = "Henderson and Velleman (1981)",            path_program    = "C:\\\\program\\\\path\\\\tables.Rmd",           path_output     = "C:\\\\output\\\\path\\\\MyTableImproved.rtf")

title: Outputting Tables
text: can also output tables column spanning headers. Let’s see works: Currently, df_to_rtf allows one level column spanning, shown example output . key details: header_table0 specifies column spanning header row. header_table1 specifies column names second header row, row directly data rows. col_widths0 col_widths1 must sum value. alignment spanning row column names largely controlled col_widths0 col_widths1 arguments. col_just0 justification column spanning header row. col_just1 justification column names row data rows. border_top1 specifies border style top border column names row; can blank, indicating top border corresponding column. happens inputs quite consistent? , col_widths0 col_widths1 sum value, header row consistent spanning row. can see , spanning header row, Performance, right border sligthly misaligned due inconsistency col_widths0 col_widths1 arguments. information inputs, check function documentation ?df_to_rtf.
code: df_to_rtf(df              = df,           file_name       = "MyColSpanTable.rtf",           org             = "My Organization",           header_page     = "My MTCARS Analysis",           table_title     = "Table 1. MTCARS Dataset",           header_table0   = c(" ", "Performance", "Engine"),           header_table1   = c("Model", "Miles Per Gallon", "Cylinders",                                "Displacement", "Horsepower"),            col_widths0     = c(1, 4, 4),           col_widths1     = c(1, 2, 2, 2, 2),           col_just0       = c("l", "c", "c"),           col_just1       = c("l", "c", "c", "c", "c"),           border_top1     = c("", "single", "single", "single", "single"),           footer_snapshot = "Henderson and Velleman (1981)",            path_program    = "C:\\\\program\\\\path\\\\tables.Rmd",           path_output     = "C:\\\\output\\\\path\\\\MyColSpanTable.rtf") df_to_rtf(df              = df,           file_name       = "MyColSpanTableProblem.rtf",           org             = "My Organization",           header_page     = "My MTCARS Analysis",           table_title     = "Table 1. MTCARS Dataset",           header_table0   = c(" ", "Performance", "Engine"),           header_table1   = c("Model", "Miles Per Gallon", "Cylinders",                                "Displacement", "Horsepower"),            col_widths0     = c(1, 4, 4),           col_widths1     = c(1, 2.2, 1.7, 2.1, 2),           col_just0       = c("l", "c", "c"),           col_just1       = c("l", "c", "c", "c", "c"),           border_top1     = c("", "single", "single", "single", "single"),           footer_snapshot = "Henderson and Velleman (1981)",            path_program    = "C:\\\\program\\\\path\\\\tables.Rmd",           path_output     = "C:\\\\output\\\\path\\\\MyColSpanTableProblem.rtf")

title: Extending 'r2rtf' for RTF Generation
text: goal r2rtfExtras provide additional helper functions assist creating RTF output r2rtf. primary wrapper functions df_to_rtf fig_to_rtf opinionated wrappers around boilerplate r2rtf code. additional capabilities currently yet built r2rtf. r2rtf package remains amazing tool generating output RTF format. Make sure read official documentation. package currently active development.

title: Extending 'r2rtf' for RTF Generation
text: package can currently installed Github repository using following: See package website details.

what: Output a dataframe to a table in RTF with opinionated formatting. — df_to_rtf
title: Output a dataframe to a table in RTF with opinionated formatting. — df_to_rtf
text: df_to_rtf convenience wrapper around r2rtf functionality   outputting RTF table, optionally least one colspan column headers.

title: Output a dataframe to a table in RTF with opinionated formatting. — df_to_rtf
code: df_to_rtf(   df,   file_name,   org,   header_page,   table_title,   header_table0,   header_table1,   col_widths0,   col_widths1,   col_just0,   col_just1,   border_top1,   footnote_table,   cell_indents,   footer_snapshot,   path_program,   path_output,   font_family = 6,   font_size = 10,   font_size_header = 10,   col_header_bg_color = "gray94",   orientation = "landscape",   margin = c(1, 1, 0.75, 0.75, 0.5, 0.5),   title_as_heading = 2,   bookmark = TRUE,   bookmark_inc_title = FALSE,   output = TRUE )

title: Output a dataframe to a table in RTF with opinionated formatting. — df_to_rtf
text: df data.frame. Dataframe output RTF. Required. file_name character. File path RTF file saved. Required. Strongly suggested include spaces file name avoid issues bookmark functionality. org character. Organization name, placed top line page header, left-aligned line right-aligned page number. Default "" (organization name). header_page character. Header text page, typically name study. table_title character. Title table, table caption. Required. header_table0 character vector. Column spanning header table. Length vector must less number columns df. Optional. header_table1 character vector. Column headers table. Length vector must equal number columns df. Required. col_widths0 numeric vector. Relative widths column spanning header. Optional. used header_table0 entered. total sum col_widths0 must equal total sum col_widths1. col_widths1 numeric vector. Relative widths column headers. col_just0 character vector. Justification column spanning header. Optional. used header_table0 entered. col_just1 character vector. Justification column headers. border_top1 character vector. Border style top border column header. Optional. Length vector must equal number columns df. footnote_table character vector. Footnotes table, . cell_indents numeric vector. Indentation cell table, twips. Default 0, indentation. cell_indents must vector length nrow(df)*ncol(df). first value controls indentation cell row 1, column 1; second value controls indentation cell row 1, column 2; . footer_snapshot character. Data snapshot used, placed top row page footer. Required. path_program character. Path program generated table, e.g., "path//program.R", placed second row footer. Required. path_output character. Path output file, e.g., "path//output.rtf", placed third row footer. entered, use file_name. font_family numeric. Font family RTF document. Default 6 (Calibri). See r2rtf:::font_type() options. font_size numeric. Font size body table, including table text, column headers, table footnote. Default 10. font_size_header numeric. Font size header footer text. Default 10. col_header_bg_color character. Background color column header rows, default "gray94". orientation character. Orientation page, either "portrait" "landscape". Default "landscape". margin numeric vector. value sets left, right, top, bottom, header footer margin order. Default c(1, 1, 0.75, 0.75, 0.5, 0.5). title_as_heading numeric. non-missing, table title inserted Word heading style. Expected input value 1, 2, 3, 4. Default 2. title style desired, set 0 specify function call. bookmark logical. TRUE, bookmark created RTF file name file (without extension). bookmark_inc_title logical. TRUE, bookmark placed RTF file title table included. Otherwise, title excluded. Default FALSE. output logical. TRUE, RTF file written location specified file_name. Otherwise, RTF object returned. Default TRUE.

code: fig_to_rtf(   plot_obj,   file_name,   org,   header_page,   figure_title,   footer_snapshot,   path_program,   path_output,   font_family = 6,   font_size_header = 10,   fig_width = 5,   fig_height = 5,   orientation = "landscape",   margin = c(1, 1, 0.75, 0.75, 0.5, 0.5),   title_as_heading = 2,   bookmark = TRUE,   bookmark_inc_title = FALSE,   output = TRUE )

title: Output a figure to RTF with opinionated formatting. — fig_to_rtf
text: plot_obj plot object. object class `ggplot` `recordedplot`. file_name character. File path RTF file saved. Required. Strongly suggested include spaces file name avoid issues bookmark functionality. org character. Organization name, placed top line page header, left-aligned line right-aligned page number. Default "" (organization name). header_page character. Header text page, typically name study. figure_title character. Title figure, figure caption. Required. footer_snapshot character. Data snapshot used, placed top row page footer. Required. path_program character. Path program generated table, e.g., "path//program.R", placed second row footer. Required. path_output character. Path output file, e.g., "path//output.rtf", placed third row footer. entered, use file_name. font_family numeric. Font family RTF document. Default 6 (Calibri). See r2rtf:::font_type() options. font_size_header numeric. Font size header footer text. Default 10. fig_width numeric. Width figure inches. Default 5. fig_height numeric. Height figure inches. Default 5. orientation character. Orientation page, either "portrait" "landscape". Default "landscape". margin numeric vector. value sets left, right, top, bottom, header footer margin order. Default c(1, 1, 0.75, 0.75, 0.5, 0.5). title_as_heading numeric. non-missing, table title inserted Word heading style. Expected input value 1, 2, 3, 4. Default 2. title style desired, set 0 specify function call. bookmark logical. TRUE, bookmark created RTF file name file (without extension). bookmark_inc_title logical. TRUE, bookmark placed RTF file title table included. Otherwise, title excluded. Default FALSE. output logical. TRUE, RTF file written location specified file_name. Otherwise, RTF object returned. Default TRUE.

title: Insert an RTF bookmark — rtf_bookmark
Insert an RTF bookmark — rtf_bookmark
text: rtf list. List 3 elements created r2rtf::rtf_encode. bookmark character. Name bookmark insert. unique name contain spaces. inc_title logical. TRUE, bookmark include table figure title. Otherwise, FALSE, title omitted. Default FALSE.

title: Insert an RTF bookmark — rtf_bookmark
text: input RTF object modified start end elements

title: Insert an RTF bookmark — rtf_bookmark
text: rtf_bookmark modifies RTF   insert bookmark start end tags within document. bookmark start tag   inserted either end RTF header (inc_title TRUE)   immediately table/figure content body (inc_title   FALSE). bookmark end tag inserted beginning RTF end element.

title: Insert an RTF bookmark — rtf_bookmark
code: # Create a simple RTF object rtf <- list(  start = "{\\rtf1\\ansi\\deff0{\\fonttbl{\\f0 Arial;}}",  body = "{\\f0\\b This is the body of the RTF document.}",  end = "}"  )   # Insert the bookmark and capture all contents rtf_bookmark(rtf       = rtf,               bookmark  = "mybookmark",               inc_title = TRUE) #> $start #> [1] "{\\rtf1\\ansi\\deff0{\\fonttbl{\\f0 Arial;}}{\\*\\bkmkstart mybookmark}" #>  #> $body #> [1] "{\\f0\\b This is the body of the RTF document.}" #>  #> $end #> [1] "{\\*\\bkmkend mybookmark}\n}" #>
